Within each row, column and box is one \'marker\' cell. Marker cells contain all digits EXCEPT 6. The row for each marker cell is indicated in row 1 of the grid. For example, if r1c2 = 9 then r9c2 is a marker. The column for each marker cell is indicated in column 1 of the grid. For example, if r3c1 = 7 then r3c7 is a marker. The position for each marker cell within a box is indicated in the lower-right corner of the box (position 9). For example, if r3c3 (lower-right for box 1) = 2, then r1c2 is a marker. Black Kropki dots in the grid indicate adjacent cells in the ratio of 2:1 (one cell is twice the other). ALL black dots are shown.'},{v:'msgcorrect: You have attained Perfect results, even in the corner, even in the spotlight.'},{v:'solution: 852396417317254986496817352749162538231785649685439271923571864168943725574628193'}],o:[{ct:[8,2.5],w:0.3,h:0.3,c1:#0,c2:#0,r:t,fs:12,te:''},{ct:[8,4.5],w:0.3,h:0.3,c1:#0,c2:#0,r:t,fs:12,te:''}],vd:[{id:'YfvasDcx94M',publishedAt:'2023-04-28T21:00:17Z',t:'The Almost Perfect Sudoku',d:2224}]}
